title: bromus tectorum subsp. longipilus
intro: Bromus tectorum subsp. longipilus, also known as cheatgrass, is a type of grass that is native to Europe and Asia but has been widely naturalized in other parts of the world, including North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Bromus tectorum subsp. longipilus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
